resources.string: Select a valid device trust certificate
Certificate selected. Tap Save to cache this certificate.
Certificate selected, but Firezone can’t use it.
Managed certificate selected, but Firezone can’t use it.
Certificate cleared. Tap Save to clear the cached certificate.
No usable device trust certificate was found at alias %1$s.
Saved certificate alias %1$s is unavailable. Select another certificate.
Managed certificate alias %1$s is unavailable. Select a valid device trust certificate.
No selectable certificates were available. If this device uses a work profile, make sure Firezone and the certificate are in the same profile.
Select a valid device trust certificate with client-auth and private-key access.
Please install a browser to sign in to Firezone.

Firezone requires the VPN permission in order to route packets from your device to protected resources in a secure manner. All communication is end-to-end encrypted; we can never decrypt or otherwise monitor your communication. Please grant the VPN permission by tapping the button below.

Firezone can send notifications to keep you informed about your connection status. This helps you know when you\'re connected to resources and if any issues occur. You can change this later in your device settings.
The token used for authentication. Set this to a service account token to enable headless operation.

A comma-separated list of application package IDs that are allowed to use the Firezone tunnel. If this list is empty, all applications are allowed. Either "Allowed Applications" OR "Disallowed Applications" may be set *but not* both.

A comma-separated list of application package IDs that are disallowed to use the Firezone tunnel and will be routed outside of it. If this list is empty, no applications are disallowed. Either "Allowed Applications" OR "Disallowed Applications" may be set *but not* both.
The name of the device. This is used to identify the device in the admin portal. If unset, device\'s model name will be used.
The base URL used for authentication. If unset, the default of https://app.firezone.dev will be used. The account slug will be appended to this URL.
The WebSocket URL used to connect to the Firezone control plane. If unset, the default of wss://api.firezone.dev will be used.
A RUST_LOG-formatted filter string used to control the verbosity of the connectivity library logging. If unset, the default of "info" will be used.

The account ID or slug of the Firezone account to authenticate to. If unset, no account slug will be used, and the user will need to enter it manually on the first sign in attempt. Upon subsequent sign ins, the account slug will be automatically populated.
Whether Firezone should automatically try to connect when the device boots up.
Whether Firezone should automatically try to connect when the app is launched.
The Android KeyChain alias of the device trust certificate Firezone should use. Intune can populate this from a certificate app configuration so users do not need to pick a certificate manually.
This setting is being managed by your organization.
